original_poem:
పుట్టుక బిడ్డకి తల్లి ఇచ్చే వరం ఐతే మరణం తల్లికి పుత్రుడు ఇచ్చే శాపం శాపం అనటం కంటే గర్భశోకం అనటం న్యాయం ఏమో దేశం కోసం మరణించే ఎంతో మంది సైనికులు తల్లులకు ఇలాంటి గర్భశోకాలని మిగులుస్తుంటే ఆ తల్బి శోకం ఈ భారతావనికి శాపంలా కాకుండా అఖండ కీర్తిని తెచ్చిపెడుతున్నాయి పేగు తెంచిన బంధం కాటికి పయనం అవుతుంటే ఉబికి వస్తున్న కంటనీరు ఆవిరైపోతుంటే దేశానికి వెలుగునిచ్చిన బిడ్డని కానరాని చీకటిలోకి సాగనంపుతూ ఆ తల్లి చెపుతుంది నా గుండెలపై సేద తీరిని నిన్ను ఈ భూమి తల్లి ఒడిలోకి పంపి నిద్రపుచ్చుతున్నా అని బిడ్డలని కన్నతల్లీ నీకు వందనం

ఆ బిడ్డలని దేశం కోసం పుడమి తల్లికి అంకితమిచ్చిన తల్లీ నీకు పాదాభివందనం..
translated_poem:
Birth is a blessing given by a mother to a child, but death is a curse given by a son to a mother, rather than a curse, it is fair to call pregnancy grief rather than a curse.
They are bringing, if the broken bond of the intestine is traveling to Kati, if the water coming to Ubi evaporates, the child who gave light to the country is dragged into the dark, the mother says, "Seda Terini on my heart, send you to the lap of this mother earth."Salutations to the mother who sees the children as sleeping

Salutations to the mother who dedicated those children for the country.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translated_lines: Birth is a blessing given by a mother to a child, but death is a curse given by a son to a mother, rather than a curse, it is fair to call pregnancy grief rather than a curse.

They are bringing, if the broken bond of the intestine is traveling to Kati, if the water coming to Ubi evaporates, the child who gave light to the country is dragged into the dark, the mother says, "Seda Terini on my heart, send you to the lap of this mother earth."Salutations to the mother who sees the children as sleeping

translated_lines: Salutations to the mother who dedicated those children for the country.

emotional_arc.value: Opens in raw maternal grief over a soldier son's death, moves through the paradox of curse and glory, and resolves in a solemn salutation to the sacrificing mother

source_lines: పుట్టుక బిడ్డకి తల్లి ఇచ్చే వరం ఐతే మరణం తల్లికి పుత్రుడు ఇచ్చే శాపం శాపం అనటం కంటే గర్భశోకం అనటం న్యాయం ఏమో దేశం కోసం మరణించే ఎంతో మంది సైనికులు తల్లులకు ఇలాంటి గర్భశోకాలని మిగులుస్తుంటే ఆ తల్బి శోకం ఈ భారతావనికి శాపంలా కాకుండా అఖండ కీర్తిని తెచ్చిపెడుతున్నాయి పేగు తెంచిన బంధం కాటికి పయనం అవుతుంటే ఉబికి వస్తున్న కంటనీరు ఆవిరైపోతుంటే దేశానికి వెలుగునిచ్చిన బిడ్డని కానరాని చీకటిలోకి సాగనంపుతూ ఆ తల్లి చెపుతుంది నా గుండెలపై సేద తీరిని నిన్ను ఈ భూమి తల్లి ఒడిలోకి పంపి నిద్రపుచ్చుతున్నా అని బిడ్డలని కన్నతల్లీ నీకు వందనం
translated_lines: Birth is a blessing given by a mother to a child, but death is a curse given by a son to a mother, rather than a curse, it is fair to call pregnancy grief rather than a curse.

They are bringing, if the broken bond of the intestine is traveling to Kati, if the water coming to Ubi evaporates, the child who gave light to the country is dragged into the dark, the mother says, "Seda Terini on my heart, send you to the lap of this mother earth."Salutations to the mother who sees the children as sleeping

loss_note.value: The Telugu phrase 'గర్భశోకం' carries the specific weight of womb-grief, untranslatable as mere pregnancy grief; 'పేగు తెంచిన బంధం' literally means the bond of severed intestines, a visceral Telugu idiom for the mother-child bond that is flattened in translation; 'సేద తీరిని' meaning one who rested and found solace on my chest is reduced to a generic phrase

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: The irreversible grief a mother carries in her womb-memory when her child dies, deeper than ordinary mourning
The biological and emotional bond between mother and child, here severed by the son's death in war
The journey to the cremation ground, used as a euphemism for death and final departure
The soldier son as a source of national light, whose death plunges the mother into personal darkness
The earth as a maternal lap receiving the dead soldier, transforming burial into a return to a greater mother

metaphor_spans.source_term: ఈ భారతావనికి శాపంలా కాకుండా అఖండ కీర్తిని తెచ్చిపెడుతున్నాయి
metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: maternal grief transformed into the nation's undying honor

metaphor_spans.source_term: దేశానికి వెలుగునిచ్చిన బిడ్డని కానరాని చీకటిలోకి సాగనంపుతూ
metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: a fallen soldier who illuminated the nation being sent into death's darkness

metaphor_spans.source_term: ఈ భూమి తల్లి ఒడిలోకి పంపి నిద్రపుచ్చుతున్నా
metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: entrusting the dead son to Mother Earth as if laying him to sleep
The stark contrast between the glory the martyr brought to the nation and the darkness of death.
Death and burial/cremation framed as returning the child to the comforting lap of Mother Earth to sleep.

visual_motifs.motif: a child who gave light to the nation being carried into unseen darkness
A grieving mother pressing her dead soldier son to her chest one last time
a mother sending her dead soldier-son into the lap of Mother Earth
A mother standing at the threshold between domestic light and the darkness into which her son disappears
A soldier's body being lowered into dark earth while his mother watches in silence
child sleeping in Mother Earth's lap
Tears rising and evaporating before they can fall, leaving the mother's face dry and hollow
tears welling up and drying as the body is taken away

source_lines: ఆ బిడ్డలని దేశం కోసం పుడమి తల్లికి అంకితమిచ్చిన తల్లీ నీకు పాదాభివందనం..
translated_lines: Salutations to the mother who dedicated those children for the country.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: The human mother who surrenders her child to the earth-mother, framing sacrifice as a sacred offering rather than a loss
offering one's children to the motherland in sacrifice
reverential bowing at the feet of the mother

visual_motifs.motif: A mother bowing her head in dignified surrender as her son's name is inscribed on a memorial
a mother dedicating her children to the motherland
bowing at a mother's feet in reverence
Folded hands offered in salutation toward a mother standing alone against an open sky

translation_note.value: Refers to the sacred land of India personified as a mother-goddess; translated generically as country, losing the devotional personification
A formal prostration at the feet as the highest mark of reverence; translated as salutations, which loses the physical act of touching feet
Translated as mother earth, but the maternal-sacral resonance is reduced.

gemini.raw_text: {"recitation_style":"reflective","emotional_arc":"From profound grief over a martyred son to deep reverence for the mother's parsed.emotional_arc: From profound grief over a martyred son to deep reverence for the mother's sacrifice.

stanzas.loss_note: The translation literally translates idioms like 'pegu tenchina bandham' (umbilical cord) as 'broken bond of the intestine' and misses the cultural nuance of 'kaatiki' (cremation ground) and 'garbhashokam' (womb-grief).

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: The deep biological and emotional bond between mother and child, symbolized by the umbilical cord.
The stark contrast between the glory the martyr brought to the nation and the darkness of death.
Death and burial/cremation framed as returning the child to the comforting lap of Mother Earth to sleep.

visual_motifs: welling tears evaporating
child sent into unseen darkness
child resting on mother's chest
child sleeping in Mother Earth's lap

stanzas.loss_note: The translation omits 'padabivandanam' (bowing to the feet) and 'pudami talliki' (to Mother Earth), losing the deep cultural reverence.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: Sacrificing one's child to the nation and earth as a sacred, selfless offering.

visual_motifs: bowing at the mother's feet

cultural_entities.translation_note: Translated literally as 'Kati' instead of the cremation ground or pyre.
Translated simply as 'salutations', losing the cultural gesture of bowing to touch the feet in reverence.
Translated as 'mother earth', capturing the personification of the earth as a mother goddess.

visual_motifs: welling tears evaporating
child sent into unseen darkness
child resting on mother's chest
child sleeping in Mother Earth's lap
bowing at the mother's feet

gemini-3-flash.raw_text: {"recitation_style":"reflective","emotional_arc":"grief to devotion","stanzas":[{"index":1,"emotion":"grief","tone":"lament","translation_quality":"partial","loss_note":"The phrase 'pegu tenchina bandham' refers to the visceral umbilical connection, which the translation renders literally as 'broken bond of the intestine', losing the emotional weight of the maternal bond.","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"పుట్టుక బిడ్డకి తల్లి ఇచ్చే వరం","abstract_meaning":"Life as a maternal gift"},{"source_term":"పేగు తెంచిన బంధం","abstract_meaning":"The inseparable biological and emotional link between mother and child"},{"source_term":"భూమి తల్లి ఒడి","abstract_meaning":"The grave as a return to nature's embrace"}],"visual_motifs":["a mother holding a child","tears evaporating into the air","the dark void of death"]},{"index":2,"emotion":"devotion","tone":"declaration","translation_quality":"faithful","loss_note":"","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"పుడమి తల్లికి అంకితం","abstract_meaning":"Sacrificing one's life for the nation's soil"}],"visual_motifs":["prostrating at a mother's feet"]}],"cultural_entities":[{"term":"భారతావని","romanization":"Bharathavani","category":"REGIONAL_SYMBOL","stanza_index":1,"preserved":true,"translation_note":"Personification of India as a mother goddess."},{"term":"పాదాభివందనం","romanization":"Padabhivandanam","category":"SOCIAL_CUSTOM","stanza_index":2,"preserved":true,"translation_note":"The act of touching feet as a mark of supreme respect."},{"term":"పుడమి తల్లి","romanization":"Pudami Talli","category":"REGIONAL_SYMBOL","stanza_index":2,"preserved":true,"translation_note":"Mother Earth, specifically the soil of the homeland."}]}

stanzas.loss_note: The phrase 'pegu tenchina bandham' refers to the visceral umbilical connection, which the translation renders literally as 'broken bond of the intestine', losing the emotional weight of the maternal bond.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: Life as a maternal gift
The inseparable biological and emotional link between mother and child
The grave as a return to nature's embrace

visual_motifs: a mother holding a child
tears evaporating into the air
the dark void of death

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: Sacrificing one's life for the nation's soil

visual_motifs: prostrating at a mother's feet

cultural_entities.translation_note: Personification of India as a mother goddess.
The act of touching feet as a mark of supreme respect.
Mother Earth, specifically the soil of the homeland.

visual_motifs: a mother holding a child
tears evaporating into the air
the dark void of death
prostrating at a mother's feet
